context: West African country with post-conflict health system. SHIP (School Health Integrated Program) implemented by Sightsavers in partnership with Ministries of Education and Health. Limited eye health workforce. NGO-dependent eye health services.

SHIP.name: School Health Integrated Program
SHIP.description: Improves health and educational outcomes of school-aged children in Liberia. Trains schoolteachers to deliver eye vision screening and provide deworming tablets in intervention areas.

key_stakeholders.coordination: Eye Health Technical Working Group (TWG) at national level

departments.education: Department of School Health under Student Personnel Services
departments.health: Department of School Health under Community Health

policy_alignment: Government of Liberia NTD Master Plan
Eye Health Policy and Strategic Plan
Nutrition, Sexual and Reproductive Health and WASH for Schools
key_challenges: Limited eye health workforce and aging health workers
Eyeglasses not on MoH Essential Supplies List
No Rehabilitation or Assistive Technology policy
Supply chain issues - glasses imported, not produced in country
Limited financial resources and competing priorities
Cultural beliefs and misconceptions about eyeglasses

training.manual: Integrated training manual covering vision screening and deworming

challenges: Most people struggle to pay for glasses
